mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns exceed +1.5% in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day forward returns in >50% of occurrences despite concurrent growth in both series.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing output, which triggers short-term equity momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of periods following positive INDPRO revisions during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 240 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >70% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and corporate profitability to generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in >55% of observations over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, driving medium-term equity appreciation and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth against 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply chain disruptions and weakening manufacturing demand, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances following the specified industrial production decline.
